---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Hayden Simm"
date: "2026-03-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

```{r}
killi_clean <- killi %>%
  drop_na(where(is.numeric))
```

PCA
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(ggfortify)
})

# Step 1: Select only relevant variables + grouping factors
killi_clean <- killi %>%
  select(
    COX_Activity,
    CS_Activity,
    LDH_Activity,
    Accliamtion_Temperature,
    Acclimation_Salinity,
    Assay_Temperature
  ) %>%
  # Remove rows with NA/Inf in the PCA variables ONLY
  filter(if_all(
    c(COX_Activity, CS_Activity, LDH_Activity),
    ~ is.finite(.)
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    group = interaction(
      Accliamtion_Temperature,
      Acclimation_Salinity,
      Assay_Temperature
    )
  )

# Step 2: Run PCA on selected variables
pca <- prcomp(
  killi_clean %>%
    select(COX_Activity, CS_Activity, LDH_Activity),
  scale. = TRUE
)

# Step 3: Plot PCA
autoplot(
  pca,
  data = killi_clean,
  colour = "Accliamtion_Temperature",
  shape  = "Acclimation_Salinity",
  frame  = TRUE,
  frame.type = "norm"
) +
  facet_wrap(~ Assay_Temperature) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Metabolic Enzyme Activity & Condition",
    colour = "Acclimation Temp",
    shape  = "Acclimation Salinity"
  )

# Step 4: Outputs
summary(pca)
pca$rotation
```

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(vegan)
})

# Step 1: Prepare data
killi_rda <- killi %>%
  select(
    Fish_ID,
    CS_Activity,
    COX_Activity,
    LDH_Activity,
    Assay_Temperature,
    Accliamtion_Temperature,
    Acclimation_Salinity
  ) %>%
  filter(if_all(
    c(CS_Activity, COX_Activity, LDH_Activity),
    ~ is.finite(.)
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    Fish_ID = factor(Fish_ID)   # <- ensure Fish_ID is treated correctly
  )

# Step 2: Response matrix
Y <- killi_rda %>%
  select(CS_Activity, COX_Activity, LDH_Activity)

# Step 3: Run partial RDA (controls for Fish_ID)
rda_model <- rda(
  Y ~ Assay_Temperature *
    Accliamtion_Temperature *
    Acclimation_Salinity +
    Condition(Fish_ID),
  data = killi_rda
)

# Step 4: Significance tests
print(summary(rda_model))
print(anova(rda_model))
print(anova(rda_model, by = "terms"))
print(anova(rda_model, by = "axis"))

# Step 5: Extract scores for plotting
sites <- as.data.frame(scores(rda_model, display = "sites", scaling = 2))
species <- as.data.frame(scores(rda_model, display = "species", scaling = 2))

# Add metadata
sites <- bind_cols(sites, killi_rda)

# Step 6: Plot (ggplot-style RDA biplot)
ggplot(sites, aes(
  x = RDA1, y = RDA2,
  colour = factor(Assay_Temperature),
  shape  = factor(Acclimation_Salinity)
)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_segment(
    data = species,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = RDA1, yend = RDA2),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = species,
    aes(x = RDA1, y = RDA2, label = rownames(species)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 1.1
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("10" = "#6BAED6", "24" = "#FB6A4A")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "RDA of Enzyme Activities",
    x = "RDA1",
    y = "RDA2",
    colour = "Assay Temp",
    shape  = "Salinity"
  )
```
